text: The surgeon begins mucosal stripping using electrocautery, starting one centimeter away from the dentate line and extending to the apex of the rectal prolapse.

The surgeon vertically plicates the underlying muscle in four quadrants using Vicryl 3-0 sutures, starting at the apex of the dissection and extending to the distal cut edge of the mucosa. The sutures are placed and tagged, with additional sutures inserted between them, totaling eight to ten sutures. As the sutures are tied, the muscle visibly plicates.
The surgeon excises the excess mucosa and performs interrupted mucosa-to-mucosa suturing using Vicryl-III-O sutures.

The surgeon performs an augmented post-anal repair through a five-centimeter curvilinear incision. Dissection is carried out into the intersphincteric space, reaching the upper part of the external anal sphincter and puborectalis muscle. The levator ani muscle and mesorectal fat are exposed, followed by the approximation of the levator ani using Prolene 2-0 sutures.
The surgeon performs skin closure using sutures to complete the procedure.
